body:
Title: CRA Form ON428 2025 Ontario resident tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5006-c/5006-c-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5006-c/5006-c-25e.pdf

CRA Form ON428 2025 calculates Ontario tax for Ontario residents. Part A calculates Ontario tax on taxable income from line 26000. The bracket thresholds are $52,886, $105,775, $150,000, and $220,000. The rates are 5.05%, 9.15%, 11.16%, 12.16%, and 13.16%. The base tax amounts are $0.00, $2,670.74, $7,510.09, $12,445.60, and $20,957.60.

Part B calculates Ontario non-refundable tax credits. Line 58040 basic personal amount is $12,747. Lines 58120 and 58160 use a base amount of $11,905 and a maximum of $10,823 for the spouse or common-law partner amount and eligible dependant amount after subtracting the spouse or dependant net income. Line 58689 medical expenses are reduced by the lesser of $2,885 and 3% of net income, then medical expenses for other dependants line 58729 are added. Line 58840 multiplies the total claim amount by 5.05%. Donations and gifts line 58969 multiplies federal Schedule 9 line 13 by 5.05% and line 14 by 11.16%. Line 61500 adds line 58840 and line 58969 as Ontario non-refundable tax credits.

Part C calculates Ontario tax. Line 53 subtracts Ontario non-refundable tax credits from Ontario tax on taxable income and floors negative amounts at zero. Line 55 adds Ontario tax on split income line 61510. Lines 56 to 61 calculate Ontario minimum tax carryover by subtracting the Ontario dividend tax credit line 61520 and limiting the carryover to 24.63% of federal minimum tax carryover line 40427. Lines 63 to 68 calculate Ontario surtax: line 66 subtracts $5,710 and multiplies by 20%, line 67 subtracts $7,307 and multiplies by 36%, and line 68 adds both components. Lines 69 to 73 add surtax, subtract the Ontario dividend tax credit, and add additional tax for minimum tax purposes.

Lines 74 to 80 calculate Ontario tax reduction when eligible. The basic reduction is $294. Dependent children and dependants with impairment use $544 each. Line 78 doubles the line 77 reduction base, and line 80 subtracts line 79 and floors negative amounts at zero. Lines 81 to 88 subtract the Ontario tax reduction, provincial foreign tax credit, LIFT credit, and 25% community food program donation credit. Line 89 calculates the Ontario health premium. The health premium thresholds are $20,000, $25,000, $36,000, $38,500, $48,000, $48,600, $72,000, $72,600, $200,000, and $200,600. The marginal rates are 6% and 25%, and fixed premiums are $300, $450, $600, $750, and $900. Line 90 adds line 88 and line 89 and enters Ontario tax on line 42800 of the T1 return. Normalized numeric values encoded from ON428 include 52886, 105775, 150000, 220000, 0.0505, 0.0915, 0.1116, 0.1216, 0.1316, 2670.74, 7510.09, 12445.60, 20957.60, 12747, 11905, 10823, 2885, 0.03, 0.2463, 5710, 7307, 0.20, 0.36, 294, 544, 0.25, 20000, 25000, 36000, 38500, 48000, 48600, 72000, 72600, 200000, 200600, 0.06, 300, 450, 600, 750, and 900.
